title: eleocharis palustris subsp. reptans
intro: Eleocharis palustris subsp. reptans, also known as the creeping spikerush, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The creeping spikerush has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, and it may also have antiviral and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
